description: You are an embedded systems engineer preparing a Micromouse robot simulation in Webots. The robot's front infrared sensor ('front_ir') is currently using a generic linear distance response, which will cause your PID wall-following controller to fail when ported to real hardware. Configure the 'front_ir' DistanceSensor on the MICROMOUSE robot to match the physical Sharp GP2Y0A41SK0F analog IR sensor's nonlinear response curve by setting the sensor type to 'infra-red' and updating the lookup table with exactly four calibration points [distance in meters, sensor ADC value, noise ratio]: [0.04, 1023, 0.0], [0.10, 800, 0.02], [0.20, 400, 0.05], and [0.30, 250, 0.08]. Save the calibrated world to /home/ga/Desktop/micromouse_calibrated.wbt.
